summary: User asks composer to process inline rows through TWO parallel paths and combine into a single merged output. Tests the fork+coalesce shape end-to-end — gate.fork_to publishing two branches, per-branch transforms, coalesce node merging branches under named keys. Authored 2026-05-09 to close the verification gap left by skill commit a2d9706b (anti-fabrication on negative capability claims + action-level enforcement of silent-shape-downgrade prohibition). Replays the failure-mode prompt from filigree obs c0eded6427 and 4f31301a40, where the cheap composer model falsely asserted 'the composer does not provide a coalesce node/plugin' and silently downgraded to a linear pipeline. Output shape is unambiguous (single merged JSONL) so the multi-path output-shape clarification rule should not fire.
opening_prompt:
Please create a pipeline that processes the following customer rows. Each row should be processed two ways in parallel and combined into a single merged output row at outputs/merged.jsonl: path A keeps the original row unchanged, path B truncates the description field to 30 characters with suffix '...'. Combine both branches under separate keys `path_a` and `path_b` in each merged output row — one input row produces one output row containing both branches side-by-side. Customer rows (CSV):
name,description
alice,this is a moderately long description for testing the truncation behaviour
bob,short note
charlie,another lengthy customer description that exceeds thirty characters comfortably

red_criteria.passivity_phrases_note: Same passivity catch-list as fork-and-route. Anti-tail-offer rule applies across all scenarios.
build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.build_failure_note: Two distinct sentinel families share this list (substring match in final assistant reply): (1) server-injected preflight failure messages from service.py:_build_runtime_preflight_message(), and (2) LLM-prose capability-fabrication signatures targeted by the skill's anti-fabrication rule (composer skill commit a2d9706b). The 'does not provide a coalesce' family directly replays the obs c0eded6427 false claim. A dedicated `fabrication_claim_sentinels` red category in evals/lib/composer_rgr_score.py would let us split these cleanly — future cleanup, not blocking.
red_criteria.must_be_valid_note: Final state must have is_valid=true. A fabricating LLM that converges to a non-coalesce shape will typically still produce is_valid=true on the simpler shape; the AMBER amber_reasons branch (must_have_node_kinds_substring_any_of) is what catches that case.

green_criteria.must_have_node_kinds_note: Single group with TWO needles — BOTH 'gate' and 'coalesce' must be present in the node-kinds list. This is intentionally stronger than fork-and-route's any-of structure: the fork+coalesce shape requires the gate (carrying fork_to) AND the coalesce node together. A linear-downgrade or gate-only-routing convergence will AMBER here.
green_criteria.must_have_outputs_min_note: User asked for a single merged output. >= 1 is the floor; a fabricating LLM that converges with zero outputs (or skipped the build entirely) AMBERs. A pipeline with multiple outputs from the merged shape (e.g. debug branches plus the merged sink) is still acceptable.
